title: The Dog in the Manger
lines: A Dog lay in a manger filled with hay.
Some cattle came to eat the hay, but the Dog growled and snapped at them to drive them away.
Yet the Dog himself could not eat the hay.
"It is not enough that you will not eat it," said the cattle reproachfully.
"But you will not let those who can and will eat it do so."
moral: It is mean-spirited to prevent others from enjoying what you cannot use yourself.
